description: Registry receipt golden vector (RFC-ACDP-0010 §5). A receipt over the sig-001 golden context, signed by a real Ed25519 registry test keypair: pins the receipt preimage canonical bytes, the receipt hash, the signature, and the producer key_fingerprint. This is the sig-001-equivalent for the receipt layer — every receipt-capable SDK pins it. Executed arithmetically by scripts/conformance-runner.py. Test keypairs MUST NOT be used in production.

registry_test_keypair.warning: TEST ONLY. The private seed is publicly known. Never use this keypair for real receipts.

producer_key.note: The sig-001 test producer key. key_fingerprint below is the RFC-ACDP-0010 §6 fingerprint of these raw bytes.

vectors.name: Receipt over the sig-001 golden context, Ed25519-signed
verification_steps: 1. JCS-canonicalize receipt_unsigned (the receipt minus 'signature'); result MUST equal expected.canonical_form.
2. SHA-256 of the canonical bytes; 'sha256:' + hex MUST equal expected.receipt_hash.
3. Sign the ASCII bytes of expected.receipt_hash with the registry test private key; base64 MUST equal expected.signature_value_base64.
4. Verify the signature with the registry test public key; verification MUST succeed.
5. SHA-256 of producer_key.public_key_hex raw bytes, prefixed 'sha256:', MUST equal receipt_unsigned.key_fingerprint (RFC-ACDP-0010 §6).

6. Cross-checks (RFC-ACDP-0010 §8): receipt fields MUST equal the sig-001 registry_assigned values and the sig-001 content_hash byte-for-byte; registry_did authority MUST equal the ctx_id/origin_registry authority.
